notes: EWSR1 ex13::NR4A3 ex2 patient-derived model. One of the two USZ models; carries the common fusion. Primary drug-screen substrate for the proteostasis-chromatin hypothesis.
TAF15::NR4A3 patient-derived model — the ONLY modern TAF15 model. Essential for fusion-subtype-stratified analysis; do not pool with USZ20-EMC1.

Third modern model (Iwata 2025). Fusion asserted as EWSR1::NR4A3 in secondary reads but NOT confirmed from primary full text (egress block) — tagged tentative. Screened against 221 agents (brigatinib, panobinostat, romidepsin = low-IC50 hits).

Classic historical EMC line. Per strategy go/no-go: use ONLY after molecular authentication of the fusion. Registered so historical results can be traced, not endorsed. Citation to be added once verified.
58/58 NR4A3-rearranged. Anchor cohort for fusion-partner frequency and TAF15-vs-EWSR1 morphology.
Variant (non-EWSR1) fusions correlate with rhabdoid phenotype / high-grade morphology.
Metastatic EWSR1::NR4A3; no recurrent secondary driver; RET expression elevated; folate-receptor in a minority.
One patient, 3 samples. EWSR1::NR4A3 is the shared founding lesion; SV burden 53(primary)->46(lung)->163(late pelvic met). Evidence for a quiet, clonal fusion + late-emerging drivers.
Public EMC expression. Reprocess raw CEL with current annotation + QC before use. Fusion-partner metadata likely absent.
Sarcoma compendium with ~6 EMC comparison tumors. Analyze on its own platform; rank-based meta-analysis across platforms, never naive matrix merge.
